items.title: Remote community love story told through first-time director's rom-com

children.content: In a First Nations romantic comedy debuting in Sydney this week, a new writer-director has used her lived experience to tell the story of young love in a remote Northern Territory community.

image.altText: A young Indigenous woman looks over her left shoulder towards a shirtless man standing behind her.

items.title: 'Like opening an oven': NT constable praised for 'heroic' rescue attempt

children.content: A "heroic" Alice Springs police officer who risked his life repeatedly entering a burning building in trying to rescue a three-year-old boy will be awarded a medal of valour.

image.altText: A shirt memorialising Mitchell Thomas that reads In loving memory of Mitchell

items.title: Couple back at Finke Desert Race after finding love via social post

children.content: When motorbike enthusiast Ben Rawcliffe posted on a Finke Desert Race Facebook group looking for his future wife, his post garnered hundreds of comments. Now, he's back in the red centre, supporting his new partner as she takes to the tough off-road track.

articles.title: Finke Desert Race legends reflect on event ahead of 50th anniversary

children.content: The 460-kilometre test of skill and endurance through the central Australian outback has become one of the most famous off-road races in the world.

articles.title: Hollywood legend set to bring Finke Desert Race to the big screen

children.content: The gruelling outback Finke Desert Race is set for Hollywood treatment after Marvel's Chris Hemsworth backs a feature-length movie.

image.altText: Chris Hemsworth stares into the camera.

gallery.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

thumbnail.altText: A man and a woman in motorbike fear in front of a range with the sun behind.

items.title: Kumanjayi Little Baby's family were forced to leave home community, MP says

children.content: Member for Lingiari Marion Scrymgour says the mother of Kumanjayi Little Baby had been living in insecure housing in Alice Springs so she could access specialist support for her daughter, before the five-year-old's tragic death in April.

image.altText: Two women sit opposite each other.

items.title: Country's biggest barra fishing comp under way, with some reporting more crocs

children.content: The country's biggest annual barramundi fishing competition is under way on the Northern Territory's remote Daly River, after the tournament was delayed by six weeks due to extensive flooding.

image.altText: White man standing on boat, wearing long sleeve purple / navy hoodie, holding barramundi fish from belly and mouth, posing

items.title: Video shows man escaping court custody in Darwin, triggering police manhunt

children.content: A manhunt is under way in Darwin as police search for Brandt Graham, a 44-year-old who allegedly scaled an enclosed courthouse dock and escaped from custody today.

image.altText: The outside of the ABC Darwin building, looking out towards the Darwin Local Court.

gallery.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

thumbnail.altText: A man in a green top running across a road, away from a court building, in a CBD.

items.title: Daly River service providers facing massive clean-up bill after record flood

children.content: Residents of the Northern Territory town of Nauiyu / Daly River have started returning home after spending three months in evacuation centres following extensive flooding.

image.altText: A man in a bright orange shirt sprays the muddy blue wall of a house

items.title: NT Supreme Court clears way for potential wave of summary adjournments

children.content: The Northern Territory Supreme Court has cleared the way for criminal defendants refused legal aid on funding grounds to have their cases adjourned indefinitely by overturning its own two-decade old ruling.

image.altText: a woman wearing glasses and a paisley dress sitting in a dark room

items.title: Cost-of-living pressure 'entrenching disadvantage' in the NT

children.content: The NT has the second highest average rent in the country, with households having to set aside more of their weekly wages to cover essential costs.

image.altText: Aerial shot of houses in Darwin

items.title: Tour operators brace for Kakadu site closures 'like COVID all over again'

children.content: Parks Australia has announced popular Kakadu attractions Jim Jim Falls and Twin Falls will remain closed for the rest of the year, with one tourism operator saying the closures will lead to a "very hard" tourism season.

image.altText: An aerial shot of Jim Jim Falls

items.title: The US ally getting nuclear-powered submarines with no AUKUS deal

children.content: South Korea is taking a different path to Australia in building nuclear-powered submarines, but its plan still hinges on the United States.

gallery.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

thumbnail.altText: Three men in white uniforms and caps salute at the entrance on top of a large grey submarine, with a South Korean flag behind.

items.title: The fake police scam stealing millions from Australia's Chinese community

children.content: Justin thought he was being investigated by the Chinese police, but was pulled into a vicious and frightening scam targeting the Chinese diaspora all over the world.

image.altText: A Chinese man with round glasses looks at a computer

items.title: Chalmers told tax changes threaten next wave of medical breakthroughs

children.content: Medical technology companies say new restrictions on tax refunds for research and development will hurt health startups in Australia.

image.altText: A person in a lab coat doing a test with a small tool.

items.title: Could a 'mansion tax' help the housing crisis?

children.content: Amid some of the most drastic proposed changes to Australia's tax system in decades and years of skyrocketing home values, one leading expert says it is an idea "worth considering".

articles.title: How worried should Sydney home owners be? Real estate agents weigh in

children.content: Sydney property prices have dipped 0.9 per cent in the past month, following a tepid six months. But how worried should home owners be?

items.title: Chris Minns has a no-frills plan to stop One Nation in NSW. Will it work?

children.content: NSW Premier Chris Minns is enjoying growing support in his electorate, but is still concerned about Pauline Hanson's presence in next year's state election.

items.title: Anthropic calls for AI freeze with fears humans risk 'losing control'

children.content: As a major AI company warns of an increased risk of "humans losing control over AI systems", the Trump administration says it will accelerate the development and use of artificial intelligence for national security applications.

image.altText: Anthropic logo is seen in this photo

items.title: Acid attack victim's lawyer describes trial of perpetrators as a 'sham'

children.content: Four military officers are facing trial over an acid attack on an Indonesian human rights campaigner that left him seriously disfigured. But some lawyers say the legal process is a "sham".

avatar.alt: Tim Swanston wearing a white shirt stands in front of a camera.

items.title: Andrew made money subletting cottages on his almost rent-free estate, watchdog finds

children.content: Former prince Andrew pocketed income from cottages on the Windsor estate while living at Royal Lodge under a lease that required virtually no rent, a watchdog report has found.

image.altText: Andrew seen through the window of a car, with a red, purplish mark on his cheek.

items.title: Anthony Head, known for roles in Buffy and Ted Lasso, dies

children.content: The British actor was well known for roles in Buffy the Vampire Slayer and Ted Lasso.

image.altText: A man smiles for the camera wearing a purple shirt and black suit jacket.

items.title: The club I wanted to join was waiting in Pompeii

children.content: A newly uncovered fresco in Pompeii tells the story of women, wine and Roman hedonism. Buried beneath the spectacle lies a lesson about restraint and why some discoveries should be left to the future.

image.altText: painted murals on a wall in an excavated building in Pompeii

avatar.alt: Virginia Trioli in a stylish suit on a set with lighting behind her

items.title: How Matt Corby overcame 19 years of fear and found magic in tragedy

children.content: He got his start as a singing TV idol, but Matt Corby is making music for himself now, finding the magic in grief, growth and family.

image.altText: White man with beard and shaggy hair poses in knit jumper againsts black and white graffiti'd wall

items.title: 'Very troubled project': Hospital cost blowout concerns SA treasurer

children.content: The new Women's and Children's Hospital was priced at $3.2 billion in 2022, before inflation and other pressures sent costs in the construction industry soaring.

items.title: Putin says he sees 'no point' meeting Zelenskyy

children.content: A Ukrainian naval drone has exploded in Romania's Black Sea port of Constanta, as European leaders warned the war in Ukraine is becoming a "direct threat" to countries along the EU's eastern border.

items.title: Even kids want gambling ads banned, so why won't the government do it?

children.content: The government's proposed crackdown on gambling ads doesn't go as far as most Australians want. When you run the numbers, it's a win for the betting industry.

items.title: Residents say public housing works have blocked them from their own yards

children.content: Neighbours of a Homes Tasmania development in Burnie say excavation works have caused their land to start slipping downhill, restricting access to parts of their property.

items.title: Mental health royal commission update silent on hundreds of promised homes

children.content: A progress report on a major mental health inquiry sparks fears that a major housing commitment has fallen to the wayside.

items.title: Lorna has lived through two world wars, two pandemics and 26 Australian prime ministers

children.content: After a couple of years as the country's oldest woman, Lorna Henstridge is still going strong.

items.title: Fox ecstatic after winning first World Cup gold since surgery

children.content: Jessica Fox is beaming with pride, having recorded her first World Cup victory since returning to international competition after surgery to remove a benign tumour from her left kidney.

items.title: Tap-and-go payments introduced to myki technology on Victorian train network

children.content: Melbourne commuters will be able to use their credit card, phone or smartwatch across the train network this month, joining other major cities with ticketless travel after repeated delays.

items.title: Bulldogs defender calls out abusive fan after fiery clash with Hawks star

items.title: Ponga injured in Knights' loss ahead of Origin II
